score.description: Strong crawlability and product-level structured data; gaps in brand-entity schema, FAQ markup, and PDP description depth. Schema hygiene issue worth attention.

criteria.title: AI crawler access
criteria.one_liner: All 8 tested AI bots allowed in robots.txt.
criteria.log_summary: AI crawler access. robots.txt has User-agent: * with path-level disallows (cart, account, search params). Only Pinterestbot blocked wholesale. All 8 tested AI bots allowed.

criteria.title: PDP server-side render
criteria.one_liner: 3/3 sampled PDPs render title, price, description in raw HTML.
criteria.log_summary: PDP server-side render. All 3 sampled PDPs render title, price, and structured description in raw HTML without JS.

criteria.title: Sitemap freshness
criteria.one_liner: Sitemap index updated 2 days before audit, 4 sub-sitemaps.
criteria.log_summary: Sitemap freshness. Valid sitemap index, 4 sub-sitemaps, <lastmod> 2 days before audit.

criteria.title: Product schema
criteria.one_liner: 3/3 PDPs ship valid Product JSON-LD with required fields. Hygiene flag below.
criteria.log_summary: Product schema. 3/3 PDPs have valid Product JSON-LD (name, image, brand, offers.price, priceCurrency, availability, sku). See hygiene flag re: duplicate malformed block.

criteria.title: AggregateRating
criteria.one_liner: Real rating values (4.5–4.7) and review counts (219–1289).
criteria.log_summary: AggregateRating. Real rating values and review counts on all 3 sampled PDPs (4.5/1289, 4.7/604, 4.6/219).

criteria.title: FAQPage / HowTo schema
criteria.one_liner: FAQ content exists but not wrapped in FAQPage JSON-LD.
criteria.log_summary: FAQPage / HowTo schema. Not present despite rich FAQ content. See detail above.

criteria.title: Organization schema
criteria.log_summary: Organization schema. Minimal block present; missing name and sameAs. See detail above.

criteria.title: PDP titles
criteria.one_liner: 5/5 sampled titles include fabric, fit, silhouette cues.
criteria.log_summary: PDP titles. 5/5 sampled titles include use-case specifics (fabric, fit, neckline, silhouette).

criteria.title: Description depth
criteria.one_liner: Spec-heavy — limited buyer context (who, when, vs what).
criteria.log_summary: PDP descriptions. Spec-heavy, limited buyer context. See detail above.

findings.title: Duplicate Product JSON-LD block is malformed

fields.label: Checked
fields.content: All 3 sampled PDPs — raw HTML via curl

fields.label: Found
fields.content: Each PDP ships two Product blocks. Block 1 is clean. Block 2 has: brand.@type set to the brand name (not "Brand"); product name mangled (e.g. "denim Denim Denim Low Rise Baggy Jeans For Daily Casual"); inconsistent priceValidUntil format; no aggregateRating.

fields.label: Why it matters
fields.content: Google's Rich Results parser and AI shopping agents may pick whichever block they encounter first. If they pick the malformed block, the product is indexed with a nonsense name and invalid brand type. Live risk, not theoretical.

fields.label: Fix
fields.content: Audit the PDP template for block 2 emission — remove if redundant, or correct @type and name. Validate a sample via Google's Rich Results Test.

fields.label: Found
fields.content: Only Organization block present. FAQ content is rich — 10 categories, dozens of questions, all server-rendered — but not wrapped in FAQPage or HowTo JSON-LD.

fields.label: Fix
fields.content: Wrap existing FAQ content in FAQPage JSON-LD in the support template. Markup layer addition, not new content work. Lightweight if Q&A is already template data. schema.org/FAQPage

fields.label: Found
fields.content: Block is emitted but only contains url + logo. Missing name ("Cider") and sameAs (Instagram / TikTok / YouTube profiles Cider operates but has not machine-linked from schema).

fields.label: Fix
fields.content: Extend the existing block with name, description, and a sameAs array. Lightweight — block already emitted; adding fields is low risk.

findings.title: PDP descriptions — spec-heavy, limited buyer context

fields.label: Checked
fields.content: All 5 sampled PDPs — rendered-response text (not source-level)

fields.label: Found
fields.content: Descriptions are spec sheets (Material, Style Details, Design, Care). Clear but lack buyer context — no "who this fit flatters", no comparison to adjacent SKUs, no "when to wear", no "what this isn't for".

fields.label: Caveat
fields.content: PDPs may include buyer-context content below the fold or in expandable sections that the default fetch didn't surface.

fields.label: Fix
fields.content: Add a "Why you'll love it" block to every PDP — who it's for, how it compares, when to wear. At Cider's scale this is a content pipeline (LLM-assisted generation from existing spec sheets is plausible), not a manual per-product rewrite.

fixes.title: Remove or repair the malformed second Product block on every PDP
fixes.description: Highest-leverage change. Cider already has clean Product + AggregateRating schema; the duplicate emission risks agents picking the wrong data. Effort depends on whether the second block comes from the same template or a separate system (e.g. a legacy SEO plugin).

fixes.title: Extend the Organization schema with name and sameAs
fixes.description: The block is already emitted site-wide on homepage and /about_us — adding ~10 lines to it would lift C7 to pass. Lightweight if the templating system exposes a single source of truth for social profiles.

fixes.title: Wrap the existing FAQ content in FAQPage JSON-LD
fixes.description: The Q&A content is already server-rendered and organized by category; the markup is the gap. Lightweight if the FAQ template has the Q&A as structured data; heavier if mixed with presentational HTML.

real_story.heading: The real story
paragraphs: Cider has stronger agentic commerce infrastructure than its reputation suggests. A fast-fashion Gen Z brand scoring 6/9 with complete Product + AggregateRating schema across the sampled catalog is genuinely above-average for the category.

The gaps are specific and fixable: missing FAQPage markup despite having the content, minimal Organization schema despite having the social footprint, and a malformed duplicate Product block that should be removed.

disclosure: This audit scored 3 PDPs and 4 auxiliary pages (homepage, /about_us, /support/faq/list, /goods/*) via source-level curl fetches on 2026-04-19. Findings are sample-based. Does not audit uniqueness (C10, deferred) or GEO (geographic/local retrieval). Schema variance in sampled PDPs does not guarantee sitewide coverage — rerun with --confidence=high or --full for broader verification.
